---
title: "Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells"
---

# Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells

Range: `1513-1582`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 70    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ----- | ----------------------------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1513  | [anomaly](#rune-1513)               | authored   | `anomaly` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the anomaly obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1514  | [backlog collapse](#rune-1514)      | authored   | `backlog collapse` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the backlog collapse obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1515  | [broken pipe](#rune-1515)           | authored   | `broken pipe` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the broken pipe obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 1516  | [bug](#rune-1516)                   | authored   | `bug` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the bug obligation named before work proceeds.                                     |
| 1517  | [cascading failure](#rune-1517)     | authored   | One failure fans out into many dependent failures.                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1518  | [checksum mismatch](#rune-1518)     | authored   | `checksum mismatch` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the checksum mismatch obligation named before work proceeds.         |
| 1519  | [collision](#rune-1519)             | authored   | `collision` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the collision obligation named before work proceeds.                         |
| 1520  | [contention](#rune-1520)            | authored   | Too many actors fighting for the same scarce resource.                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1521  | [corruption](#rune-1521)            | authored   | Stored or transmitted truth damaged into falsehood.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| 1522  | [crash](#rune-1522)                 | authored   | `crash` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the crash obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1523  | [curse of shared state](#rune-1523) | authored   | `curse of shared state` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the curse of shared state obligation named before work proceeds. |
| 1524  | [data drift](#rune-1524)            | authored   | `data drift` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the data drift obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1525  | [deadlock {Curse}](#rune-1525)      | authored   | Two or more actors wait on one another forever.                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 1526  | [defect](#rune-1526)                | authored   | `defect` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the defect obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1527  | [degraded mode](#rune-1527)         | authored   | `degraded mode` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the degraded mode obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 1528  | [denial of service](#rune-1528)     | authored   | `denial of service` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the denial of service obligation named before work proceeds.         |
| 1529  | [dependency hell](#rune-1529)       | authored   | Transitive requirements turning ordinary change into a trap.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 1530  | [divergence](#rune-1530)            | authored   | `divergence` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the divergence obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1531  | [drift](#rune-1531)                 | authored   | Slow departure from the world you thought you were still in.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 1532  | [error](#rune-1532)                 | authored   | `error` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the error obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1533  | [exception](#rune-1533)             | authored   | `exception` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the exception obligation named before work proceeds.                         |
| 1534  | [exhaustion](#rune-1534)            | authored   | The resource is simply used up.                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 1535  | [failure domain](#rune-1535)        | authored   | `failure domain` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the failure domain obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 1536  | [fault](#rune-1536)                 | authored   | `fault` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the fault obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1537  | [flapping](#rune-1537)              | authored   | Rapid unhealthy oscillation between states.                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| 1538  | [fragmentation](#rune-1538)         | authored   | `fragmentation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the fragmentation obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 1539  | [freeze](#rune-1539)                | authored   | `freeze` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the freeze obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1540  | [hang](#rune-1540)                  | authored   | `hang` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the hang obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 1541  | [hotspot](#rune-1541)               | authored   | `hotspot` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the hotspot obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1542  | [inconsistent read](#rune-1542)     | authored   | A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.                                                                                                                                                             |
| 1543  | [infinite loop](#rune-1543)         | authored   | `infinite loop` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the infinite loop obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 1544  | [invalid state](#rune-1544)         | authored   | `invalid state` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the invalid state obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 1545  | [jitter spike](#rune-1545)          | authored   | `jitter spike` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the jitter spike obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 1546  | [leak {Curse}](#rune-1546)          | authored   | Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.                                                                                                           |
| 1547  | [livelock](#rune-1547)              | authored   | Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| 1548  | [lost update](#rune-1548)           | authored   | One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 1549  | [memory corruption](#rune-1549)     | authored   | `memory corruption` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the memory corruption obligation named before work proceeds.         |
| 1550  | [mismatch](#rune-1550)              | authored   | `mismatch` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the mismatch obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 1551  | [misroute](#rune-1551)              | authored   | `misroute` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the misroute obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 1552  | [missed heartbeat](#rune-1552)      | authored   | `missed heartbeat` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the missed heartbeat obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1553  | [null dereference](#rune-1553)      | authored   | `null dereference` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the null dereference obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1554  | [outage](#rune-1554)                | authored   | Service unavailable where availability was expected.                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 1555  | [overflow](#rune-1555)              | authored   | Value exceeds the representable range.                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1556  | [packet loss {Curse}](#rune-1556)   | authored   | `packet loss` in its Curse sense is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the packet loss obligation named before work proceeds.  |
| 1557  | [panic](#rune-1557)                 | authored   | `panic` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the panic obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1558  | [partial failure](#rune-1558)       | authored   | Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1559  | [partition storm](#rune-1559)       | authored   | `partition storm` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the partition storm obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 1560  | [poison pill](#rune-1560)           | authored   | `poison pill` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the poison pill obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 1561  | [race](#rune-1561)                  | authored   | An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.                                                                                                       |
| 1562  | [regression](#rune-1562)            | authored   | A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| 1563  | [resource starvation](#rune-1563)   | authored   | `resource starvation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the resource starvation obligation named before work proceeds.     |
| 1564  | [retry storm](#rune-1564)           | authored   | Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.                                                                                                         |
| 1565  | [rollback failure](#rune-1565)      | authored   | `rollback failure` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the rollback failure obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1566  | [saturation](#rune-1566)            | authored   | `saturation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the saturation obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1567  | [schema drift](#rune-1567)          | authored   | The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.                                                                                        |
| 1568  | [split-brain {Curse}](#rune-1568)   | authored   | Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1569  | [stack overflow](#rune-1569)        | authored   | `stack overflow` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stack overflow obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 1570  | [stale read](#rune-1570)            | authored   | `stale read` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stale read obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1571  | [stall](#rune-1571)                 | authored   | `stall` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stall obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1572  | [starvation](#rune-1572)            | authored   | `starvation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the starvation obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1573  | [state explosion](#rune-1573)       | authored   | `state explosion` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the state explosion obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 1574  | [storm](#rune-1574)                 | authored   | `storm` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the storm obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1575  | [thrash](#rune-1575)                | authored   | `thrash` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the thrash obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1576  | [timeout {Curse}](#rune-1576)       | authored   | `timeout` in its Curse sense is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the timeout obligation named before work proceeds.          |
| 1577  | [torn write](#rune-1577)            | authored   | `torn write` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the torn write obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1578  | [underflow](#rune-1578)             | authored   | `underflow` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the underflow obligation named before work proceeds.                         |
| 1579  | [unhandled exception](#rune-1579)   | authored   | `unhandled exception` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the unhandled exception obligation named before work proceeds.     |
| 1580  | [unstable equilibrium](#rune-1580)  | authored   | `unstable equilibrium` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the unstable equilibrium obligation named before work proceeds.   |
| 1581  | [write amplification](#rune-1581)   | authored   | `write amplification` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the write amplification obligation named before work proceeds.     |
| 1582  | [write skew {Curse}](#rune-1582)    | authored   | `write skew` in its Curse sense is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the write skew obligation named before work proceeds.    |

## Entries

### [1513] anomaly {#rune-1513}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `anomaly` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the anomaly obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `anomaly` at rune 1513 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1514] backlog collapse {#rune-1514}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `backlog collapse` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the backlog collapse obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `backlog collapse` at rune 1514 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1515] broken pipe {#rune-1515}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `broken pipe` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the broken pipe obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `broken pipe` at rune 1515 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1516] bug {#rune-1516}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `bug` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the bug obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `bug` at rune 1516 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1517] cascading failure {#rune-1517}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** One failure fans out into many dependent failures.

**Shadow:** misusing `cascading failure` at rune 1517 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** One failure fans out into many dependent failures.

### [1518] checksum mismatch {#rune-1518}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `checksum mismatch` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the checksum mismatch obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `checksum mismatch` at rune 1518 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1519] collision {#rune-1519}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `collision` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the collision obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `collision` at rune 1519 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1520] contention {#rune-1520}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Too many actors fighting for the same scarce resource.

**Shadow:** misusing `contention` at rune 1520 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Too many actors fighting for the same scarce resource.

### [1521] corruption {#rune-1521}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Stored or transmitted truth damaged into falsehood.

**Shadow:** misusing `corruption` at rune 1521 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Stored or transmitted truth damaged into falsehood.

### [1522] crash {#rune-1522}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `crash` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the crash obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `crash` at rune 1522 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1523] curse of shared state {#rune-1523}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `curse of shared state` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the curse of shared state obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `curse of shared state` at rune 1523 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1524] data drift {#rune-1524}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `data drift` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the data drift obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `data drift` at rune 1524 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1525] deadlock {Curse} {#rune-1525}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Two or more actors wait on one another forever.

**Shadow:** misusing `deadlock` at rune 1525 in the Curse lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Two or more actors wait on one another forever.

### [1526] defect {#rune-1526}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `defect` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the defect obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `defect` at rune 1526 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1527] degraded mode {#rune-1527}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `degraded mode` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the degraded mode obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `degraded mode` at rune 1527 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1528] denial of service {#rune-1528}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `denial of service` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the denial of service obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `denial of service` at rune 1528 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1529] dependency hell {#rune-1529}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Transitive requirements turning ordinary change into a trap.

**Shadow:** misusing `dependency hell` at rune 1529 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Transitive requirements turning ordinary change into a trap.

### [1530] divergence {#rune-1530}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `divergence` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the divergence obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `divergence` at rune 1530 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1531] drift {#rune-1531}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Slow departure from the world you thought you were still in.

**Shadow:** misusing `drift` at rune 1531 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Slow departure from the world you thought you were still in.

### [1532] error {#rune-1532}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `error` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the error obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `error` at rune 1532 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1533] exception {#rune-1533}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `exception` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the exception obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `exception` at rune 1533 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1534] exhaustion {#rune-1534}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The resource is simply used up.

**Shadow:** misusing `exhaustion` at rune 1534 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** The resource is simply used up.

### [1535] failure domain {#rune-1535}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `failure domain` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the failure domain obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `failure domain` at rune 1535 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1536] fault {#rune-1536}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `fault` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the fault obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `fault` at rune 1536 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1537] flapping {#rune-1537}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Rapid unhealthy oscillation between states.

**Shadow:** misusing `flapping` at rune 1537 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Rapid unhealthy oscillation between states.

### [1538] fragmentation {#rune-1538}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `fragmentation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the fragmentation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `fragmentation` at rune 1538 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1539] freeze {#rune-1539}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `freeze` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the freeze obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `freeze` at rune 1539 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1540] hang {#rune-1540}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `hang` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the hang obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `hang` at rune 1540 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1541] hotspot {#rune-1541}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `hotspot` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the hotspot obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `hotspot` at rune 1541 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1542] inconsistent read {#rune-1542}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.

**Shadow:** misusing `inconsistent read` at rune 1542 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.

### [1543] infinite loop {#rune-1543}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `infinite loop` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the infinite loop obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `infinite loop` at rune 1543 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1544] invalid state {#rune-1544}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `invalid state` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the invalid state obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `invalid state` at rune 1544 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1545] jitter spike {#rune-1545}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `jitter spike` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the jitter spike obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `jitter spike` at rune 1545 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1546] leak {Curse} {#rune-1546}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.

**Shadow:** when treating a leak as the cause prevents finding the owner, lifetime, or boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Something escapes the boundary that should have held it.

### [1547] livelock {#rune-1547}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.

**Shadow:** misusing `livelock` at rune 1547 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.

### [1548] lost update {#rune-1548}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.

**Shadow:** misusing `lost update` at rune 1548 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.

### [1549] memory corruption {#rune-1549}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `memory corruption` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the memory corruption obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `memory corruption` at rune 1549 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1550] mismatch {#rune-1550}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `mismatch` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the mismatch obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `mismatch` at rune 1550 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1551] misroute {#rune-1551}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `misroute` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the misroute obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `misroute` at rune 1551 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1552] missed heartbeat {#rune-1552}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `missed heartbeat` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the missed heartbeat obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `missed heartbeat` at rune 1552 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1553] null dereference {#rune-1553}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `null dereference` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the null dereference obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `null dereference` at rune 1553 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1554] outage {#rune-1554}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Service unavailable where availability was expected.

**Shadow:** misusing `outage` at rune 1554 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Service unavailable where availability was expected.

### [1555] overflow {#rune-1555}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Value exceeds the representable range.

**Shadow:** misusing `overflow` at rune 1555 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Value exceeds the representable range.

### [1556] packet loss {Curse} {#rune-1556}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `packet loss` in its Curse sense is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the packet loss obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `packet loss` at rune 1556 in the Curse lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1557] panic {#rune-1557}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `panic` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the panic obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `panic` at rune 1557 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1558] partial failure {#rune-1558}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.

**Shadow:** misusing `partial failure` at rune 1558 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.

### [1559] partition storm {#rune-1559}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `partition storm` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the partition storm obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `partition storm` at rune 1559 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1560] poison pill {#rune-1560}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `poison pill` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the poison pill obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `poison pill` at rune 1560 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1561] race {#rune-1561}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.

**Shadow:** when the race is patched locally while the shared state contract remains undefined.

**Pocket gloss:** Outcome depends on timing you do not actually control.

### [1562] regression {#rune-1562}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.

**Shadow:** misusing `regression` at rune 1562 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.

### [1563] resource starvation {#rune-1563}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `resource starvation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the resource starvation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `resource starvation` at rune 1563 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1564] retry storm {#rune-1564}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.

**Shadow:** when retries synchronize into an outage amplifier rather than a recovery path.

**Pocket gloss:** Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it.

### [1565] rollback failure {#rune-1565}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `rollback failure` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the rollback failure obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `rollback failure` at rune 1565 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1566] saturation {#rune-1566}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `saturation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the saturation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `saturation` at rune 1566 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1567] schema drift {#rune-1567}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.

**Shadow:** when schema changes are shipped without compatibility checks, migration windows, or readers.

**Pocket gloss:** Stored shape and assumed shape have silently diverged.

### [1568] split-brain {Curse} {#rune-1568}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.

**Shadow:** misusing `split-brain` at rune 1568 in the Curse lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.

### [1569] stack overflow {#rune-1569}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `stack overflow` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stack overflow obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `stack overflow` at rune 1569 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1570] stale read {#rune-1570}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `stale read` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stale read obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `stale read` at rune 1570 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1571] stall {#rune-1571}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `stall` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stall obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `stall` at rune 1571 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1572] starvation {#rune-1572}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `starvation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the starvation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `starvation` at rune 1572 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1573] state explosion {#rune-1573}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `state explosion` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the state explosion obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `state explosion` at rune 1573 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1574] storm {#rune-1574}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `storm` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the storm obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `storm` at rune 1574 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1575] thrash {#rune-1575}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `thrash` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the thrash obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `thrash` at rune 1575 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1576] timeout {Curse} {#rune-1576}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `timeout` in its Curse sense is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the timeout obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `timeout` at rune 1576 in the Curse lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1577] torn write {#rune-1577}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `torn write` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the torn write obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `torn write` at rune 1577 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1578] underflow {#rune-1578}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `underflow` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the underflow obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `underflow` at rune 1578 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1579] unhandled exception {#rune-1579}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `unhandled exception` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the unhandled exception obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `unhandled exception` at rune 1579 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1580] unstable equilibrium {#rune-1580}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `unstable equilibrium` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the unstable equilibrium obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `unstable equilibrium` at rune 1580 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1581] write amplification {#rune-1581}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `write amplification` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the write amplification obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `write amplification` at rune 1581 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1582] write skew {Curse} {#rune-1582}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `write skew` in its Curse sense is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the write skew obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `write skew` at rune 1582 in the Curse lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.
